questions.question:
An application in namespace `backend` needs to call a service in namespace `frontend` via DNS. The service name is `web-ui` and it exposes port 3000.

The application's configuration needs the full DNS name to connect. What is the correct DNS name within the cluster that the backend application should use?

Write the exact DNS name to a file `/tmp/exam/q1_dns_name.txt`.

Also, verify that pod-to-pod DNS resolution works using nslookup or dig from a test pod.

A deployment `payment-service` has been updated with a new image version that introduced a bug. Users are reporting errors. You need to:
1. Check the deployment's rollout history
2. Identify which revision had the issue
3. Rollback to the previous stable version
4. Verify the rollback completed successfully

A pod needs access to database credentials:
- Username: `dbuser`
- Password: `secret123`
- Database: `appdb`

Create a Secret named `db-creds` and mount it in a deployment named `app-deploy` as environment variables (not as files).

Verify that the pod can access these environment variables without exposing them in pod logs.

verification.description: Deployment mounts secret as env vars

questions.question:
A monitoring agent needs to run on every node in the cluster, including control plane nodes. Currently, some nodes are missing the agent due to taints.

Create or update a DaemonSet named `monitoring-agent` to:
1. Run the `datadog/agent:latest` image on all nodes
2. Tolerate the taint `node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule`
3. Verify the agent is running on all nodes

The API server appears to be responding slowly. You need to:
1. Check the API server logs for errors or warnings
2. Verify the API server is listening on port 6443

Write the command you would use to check the API server logs to a file `/tmp/exam/q5_command.txt`.

On a kubeadm-managed control plane, etcd runs as a static pod and its certificates live under `/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/`.

Write the full `etcdctl snapshot save` command you would use to back up etcd to `/tmp/etcd-backup.db` to the file `/tmp/exam/q6_etcd_backup.txt`. The command must include the `--endpoints`, `--cacert`, `--cert`, and `--key` flags.

verification.description: Command contains etcdctl snapshot save with all required flags

questions.question:
1. Taint the node `k3d-cluster-agent-0` with `dedicated=special:NoSchedule`
2. Create a pod named `toleration-pod` (image `nginx`) in the `default` namespace that **tolerates** this taint
3. Verify the pod runs

Create a pod named `init-demo` in the `default` namespace with:

1. A shared `emptyDir` volume named `work`
2. An **initContainer** (image `busybox`) that writes the word `hello` into `/work/index.html`
3. A main container (image `nginx`) that mounts the `work` volume at `/usr/share/nginx/html`

verification.description: Pod has an emptyDir volume named 'work'

questions.question:
1. Create a ServiceAccount named `build-sa` in the `default` namespace
2. Create a pod named `sa-pod` (image `nginx`) in `default` that runs as the `build-sa` ServiceAccount

1. Create a PriorityClass named `high-prio` with value `1000000` (it must **not** be the global default)
2. Create a pod named `prio-pod` (image `nginx`) in `default` that uses this PriorityClass

1. Create a PersistentVolumeClaim named `data-pvc` in `default` requesting `1Gi` with access mode `ReadWriteOnce`, using the cluster's **default** StorageClass (`local-path`)
2. Create a pod named `pvc-pod` (image `nginx`) that mounts this PVC at `/data`
3. Confirm the PVC becomes `Bound`

A Deployment named `frontend` in `default` never becomes ready: its `readinessProbe` targets port `8080`, but nginx serves on port `80`.

Fix the readinessProbe to use port `80` so the Deployment becomes available.

verification.description: Deployment frontend has an available replica

questions.question:
A Deployment `cfg-app` in namespace `apps` will not start: its pods are stuck in `CreateContainerConfigError` because it references a ConfigMap `app-cfg` (via `envFrom`) that does not exist.

Create the missing ConfigMap `app-cfg` in `apps` with the key `MODE=prod` so the Deployment becomes available.

verification.description: Deployment cfg-app has an available replica

questions.question:
A Deployment `web2` (nginx, serves on port 80) in `default` is exposed by Service `web2-svc`, but requests fail because the Service's `targetPort` points at the wrong port (`8080`).

Fix `web2-svc` so it targets port `80`.

In namespace `production`, create a NetworkPolicy named `backend-allow` that allows **ingress** to pods labelled `app=backend` on TCP port `8080`, but **only** from pods that are simultaneously:

- in a namespace labelled `env=prod`, **AND**
- labelled `app=frontend`

This must be a single combined `from` entry (namespaceSelector **and** podSelector together), not two separate entries.

> The cluster CNI (flannel) does not enforce NetworkPolicy, so this is graded on the policy **spec**.

verification.description: Single from-entry ANDs namespaceSelector and podSelector

questions.question:
Expose the Deployment `np-app` in `default` with a **NodePort** Service named `np-svc`:

- Service port `80`
- Target port `80`
- NodePort **exactly** `30080`

verification.description: NodePort Service np-svc on port 80 with nodePort 30080

questions.question:
Write the ordered list of commands to upgrade the **kubelet** on a **worker** node to `v1.33.1` on a kubeadm cluster, to the file `/tmp/exam/q17_upgrade.txt`. Your answer must include: draining the node, `kubeadm upgrade node`, upgrading and restarting kubelet, and uncordoning the node.

> This cluster is k3d, so you recall the command sequence rather than running it.

verification.description: Upgrade sequence includes drain, kubeadm upgrade node, kubelet restart, uncordon
